must_detect.matched_text:
Aurora Harbour Technology Limited (CR No. 81234567; Stock Code: 08765, HKEX Main Board) reports an operational cyber incident detected on 02 June 2026.
A phishing-led credential compromise accessed a shared drive containing draft M&A board papers for “Project Lantern” regarding a potential acquisition of Pearl Orion Holdings Limited.
The materials constitute inside information under Part XIVA of the SFO, not generally known to persons accustomed to dealing in securities and potentially price-sensitive.
Preliminary forensics indicate exfiltration of an employee subfolder with PDPO-regulated data, including HKID scans and two passport entries (e.g., HKSAR Passport K1234563 – checksum invalid).
One sample record lists Mr. Desmond Yau, Finance Manager (HKID: A823456(0) – checksum invalid), payroll email dyau.finance@auroraharbour.com.hk, and office line +852 2987 1234.
Another record lists Ms. Clara Poon (HKSAR Passport No. T9382715), MPF Scheme ID APB-77-4521, and address Unit 2307, 23/F, Harbour Apex, 88 Connaught Road Central, Hong Kong.
A vendor contact sheet also reflects Orchid Bay Capital SPC – Sub‑fund A (Fund Ref: OBC‑SFA‑2026), diligence coordinator email dd@orchidbaycap.asia, and bank settlement A/C 713‑452‑123456‑001 at Harbour Prosperity Bank (HK) Ltd.
The HR folder included offer letters and IR56E templates; certain generic placeholders (Name: __________; Account No.: __________) were present and do not identify any individual.
We note a misfiled public service number “1823” in the emergency contact column; this is a government helpline and not a company phone.
No customer retail data, payment card numbers, or system credentials for production trading were accessed; core order routing and OMS remained segregated.
The incident was contained within 4 hours; compromised credentials were reset and SFC-licensed staff were notified under internal compliance policy CP‑SFC‑04.
We have notified HKEX under Main Board Listing Rule 13.09 and are assessing whether a trading halt is required if MNPI public disclosure becomes necessary.
External counsel, Jade Meridian Chambers, has advised preservation of evidence and PDPO breach notification steps; queries may be directed to legal@auroraharbour.com.hk.
For investors, the designated IR channel is ir@auroraharbour.com.hk; do not use the general hotline listed on forms, and do not contact “the Compliance Officer” role alias for media enquiries.
Temporary access to the data room for Orchid Bay Capital SPC is suspended; revised NDAs will require explicit carve-outs for cyber incidents and employment records retention.
Affected employees will receive individual notices at registered emails and via HR line +852 3765 4402; no deduction from MPF or payroll accounts will occur.
Registered office: 28/F, Starview Tower, 3 Sheung Wan Road, Hong Kong; tax BR No. 20334455-000-12-6 (for reference only, not a bank identifier).
This notice is confidential and intended for board and SFC compliance review; unauthorized dealing based on the above may contravene the SFO.
